heaviest_rainfall.status: The most intense rainfall recorded took place on 09 November 2025, with precipitation totalling 47.89mm.  The rain fell at 24.6mm per hour at times over the course of the 144.8 hour storm.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 02 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently a cold snap underway.  It started on 2026-01-01 and has lasted 6 day.  So far this year, there has been 1 cold snap.  Typically, the yearly average is 3.6.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 0 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (195°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The record wind gust remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

run.status: The record wind run remains at 1737 miles from 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The highest wind speed ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 3.4°C), outside temperature (down 3.4°C), and inside temperature (down 2.6°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 106 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 5.0°C), inside temperature (up 6.0°C), and cloud levels (up 108 m) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 106.2m lower than yesterday, 108.0m lower than the past week, 99.9m lower than the past month.

Yesterday, the cloud levels ranged from a minimum of 845m to a maximum of 1717m, averaging 1092.5m; the past week and month had slightly higher averages of 1200.5m and 1192.3m respectively.

summary.summary: Inside; prepare for cold weather. Outside; bitterly cold temperatures are expected. Also, the air will feel dry and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, occasional overcast conditions, and light levels will be low during the day. Weather patterns to stay the same.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (2.1°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
